Internal Compliance Memo — Falcon Dunes PJSC (ADGM branch) — PDPL/DPR alignment and market conduct safeguards
This note summarises Q2 privacy handling controls for UAE operations and confirms that capital markets materials reference only SCA/ADX/DFM public sources, not non-public issuer information.
DPA Schedule 2 (Data Export Map) records cross-border transfer UAEX-SIN-2026-07 approved on 2026-04-18 for HR onboarding files under ADGM standard contractual clauses with Cloud Quarterdeck Pte; retention set to 90 days post-onboarding then deletion.
No live transfers are pending, and all subsequent exports remain blocked by default until Data Export Approval forms are countersigned by Legal and the Data Protection Officer.
Onboarding Form v3.4 (Mainland + DIFC/ADGM) applies data minimisation: Emirates ID is masked to last 3 digits only, trade licence is collected from corporates, and the optional “family origin” field has been removed.
Template labels are illustrative in the form pack: Emirates ID 784-XXXX-XXXXXXX-X (placeholder), Trade Licence TL-0000-XX, ADGM Reg. No. ADGM-00-00000; these examples are not customer data and should not be populated in production.
Consent withdrawal remains enabled via privacy.office@falcondunes.example; there are no open withdrawals as of 2026-05-30 and the last completed withdrawal has confirmation and deactivation logs archived.
One DSAR received on 2026-05-02 for a former contractor was actioned and erasure completed on 2026-05-10 across CRM, HRIS, and backups, with a PDPL rights response issued within statutory timelines.
Retention schedule DPR-ADGM-RET-2025 applies 6 months for onboarding documentation and 5 years for statutory finance records; automated deletion jobs have passed verification, and no retention exceptions are active.
The DIFC branch follows DP Law 2020 controls; adequacy mapping confirms no onward transfers outside approved jurisdictions without a signed Data Export Approval, and no ad hoc cloud sync is permitted.
Compliance confirms that issuer-related folders in the Capital Markets drive contain only ADX/DFM announcements and SCA circulars; no MNPI is stored in shared drives or deal rooms.
Investor relations scripts reference public, already-announced disclosures; an internal draft investor Q&A with stale content is quarantined for training and contains no market-sensitive numbers.
DPA Schedule 4 (Sub-Processor List) is unchanged; the prior removal of “Blue Oasis Analytics KSA” from the vendor stack is complete and the closure ticket is on file.
Data subject access is available through the web portal; paper forms are not accepted, and role mailbox escalations are tracked under PDPL-REQ-YYYY-#### placeholders for consistency.
This memo records completed controls only and does not authorise collection beyond the minimum necessary for onboarding and regulatory reporting under PDPL and ADGM/DIFC requirements.
